skill.name: test-generator
skill.description: Generate pytest boilerplate for Python modules, classes, functions, async functions, parametrized cases, and fixtures using project TDD conventions.
skill.summary: Create structured pytest test boilerplate with type hints, docstrings, fixtures, and AAA test layout.
skill.author: Atyantik

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby backticks, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance were reviewed and are false positives from Markdown code fences, placeholders, and test examples. The skill has low operational risk because it reads project files, writes generated test files, and recommends local test commands, all aligned with its stated purpose.

low_findings.title: Expected Filesystem Access for Test Generation
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to read source files and project documentation, then save generated tests. This is necessary for a test generator, but users should review file paths before accepting changes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly require reading source files and saving test files. The behavior is expected for the skill and no exfiltration or destructive intent was found.

low_findings.title: Local Command Guidance Requires Review
low_findings.description: The skill recommends running local commands such as make test, make coverage, make format, and a Python generator script. These commands are normal development actions, but execution depends on the host project.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in the skill documentation and are development-oriented. Risk remains low because there is no hidden command construction, obfuscation, or network transfer.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown and Templates
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution, weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance findings were reviewed as Markdown fences, prose, imports, placeholders, or test examples. No evidence found of malicious execution, weak crypto implementation, or reconnaissance logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows the flagged lines are documentation blocks and inert pytest boilerplate. They do not contain executable shell backticks, crypto routines, system probes, or network calls.

content.value_statement: Writing consistent pytest scaffolding takes time and can drift from team standards. This skill creates structured test files, fixtures, and parametrized cases that follow project TDD conventions.
actual_capabilities: Creates pytest boilerplate for functions, classes, async functions, and fixtures.
Uses AAA structure with arrange, act, and assert sections.
Adds type hints and Google-style docstrings to generated tests.
Suggests parametrized cases for valid inputs, invalid inputs, and edge cases.
Produces summaries of generated test classes, functions, fixtures, and next steps.
limitations: Generated TODO sections still require project-specific test data and assertions.
It depends on accessible source files or a clear user description.
It does not guarantee runtime correctness without running the project test suite.
It is tailored to pytest and Python project conventions.

use_cases.title: Start Tests for a New Module
use_cases.description: Generate a first pytest file for a new Python module with imports, test classes, and edge case placeholders.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Test Structure
use_cases.description: Create boilerplate that follows shared TDD conventions, type hints, docstrings, and AAA layout.

use_cases.title: Add Async and Parametrized Coverage
use_cases.description: Prepare async test cases and parametrized inputs for functions with multiple scenarios.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Basic Function Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate pytest tests for the validate_email function. Include happy path, invalid input, and edge case placeholders.

prompt_templates.title: Create Tests from a Source File
prompt_templates.scenario: Use an existing module as context
prompt_templates.prompt: Read src/python_modern_template/validators.py and generate a pytest file for its public functions using project conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Build Class Test Boilerplate
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate tests for a class with methods
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate tests for a UserManager class with create_user, delete_user, and find_user methods. Include a fixture for the class instance.

prompt_templates.title: Design Parametrized Async Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate async pytest tests for fetch_data(url: str) -> dict. Include timeout, error response, and concurrent execution scenarios.

output_examples.input: Generate tests for a simple email validator.
output_examples.output: A pytest test file outline with a validator test class, valid email cases, invalid email cases, and edge case TODOs.

output_examples.input: Generate tests for a service class with create and delete methods.
output_examples.output: A class-focused test outline with an instance fixture, initialization checks, method tests, and error handling placeholders.

output_examples.input: Generate async tests for a data fetch function.
output_examples.output: An async pytest outline with asyncio markers, basic result checks, failure scenarios, and concurrent call coverage.

best_practices: Review every generated TODO and replace it with real inputs and assertions.
Run generated tests before implementation when following TDD.
Use parametrized tests when one behavior needs many input combinations.
anti_patterns: Do not accept placeholder assertions as final coverage.
Do not generate tests for private implementation details unless the project requires it.
Do not run project commands without reviewing what they execute.

faq.question: Does this skill write complete tests?
faq.answer: It creates strong boilerplate, but users must add real data, expected values, and final assertions.

faq.question: Which test framework does it target?
faq.answer: It targets pytest for Python projects.

faq.question: Can it generate async tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes async pytest structure and concurrent execution examples.

faq.question: Can it create fixtures?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a fixture template for reusable test resources.

faq.question: Does it follow TDD?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow encourages generating tests first, running them, then implementing code.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found low operational risk and no evidence of malicious behavior.
